verses.latin_vulgate: Quod factum est verbum Domini ad Jeremiam prophetam contra Palæstinos, antequam percuteret Pharao Gazam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֨ר הָיָ֧ה דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶל־יִרְמְיָ֥הוּ הַנָּבִ֖יא אֶל־פְּלִשְׁתִּ֑ים בְּטֶ֛רֶם יַכֶּ֥ה פַרְעֹ֖ה אֶת־עַזָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: The word of the Lord that came to Jeremiah the prophet against the Philistines, before that Pharaoh smote Gaza.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Ecce aquæ ascendunt ab aquilone,  et erunt quasi torrens inundans,  et operient terram et plenitudinem ejus,  urbem et habitatores ejus.  Clamabunt homines,  et ululabunt omnes habitatores terræ,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה הִנֵּה־מַ֜יִם עֹלִ֤ים מִצָּפוֹן֙ וְהָיוּ֙ לְנַ֣חַל שׁוֹטֵ֔ף וְיִשְׁטְפוּ֙ אֶ֣רֶץ וּמְלוֹאָ֔הּ עִ֖יר וְיֹ֣שְׁבֵי בָ֑הּ וְזָעֲקוּ֙ הָאָדָ֔ם וְהֵילִ֕ל כֹּ֖ל יוֹשֵׁ֥ב הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord; Behold, waters rise up out of the north, and shall be an overflowing flood, and shall overflow the land, and all that is therein; the city, and them that dwell therein: then the men shall cry, and all the inhabitants of the land shall howl.

verses.latin_vulgate: a strepitu pompæ armorum, et bellatorum ejus,  a commotione quadrigarum ejus, et multitudine rotarum illius.  Non respexerunt patres filios manibus dissolutis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִקּ֗וֹל שַֽׁעֲטַת֙ פַּרְס֣וֹת אַבִּירָ֔יו מֵרַ֣עַשׁ לְרִכְבּ֔וֹ הֲמ֖וֹן גַּלְגִּלָּ֑יו לֹא־הִפְנ֤וּ אָבוֹת֙ אֶל־בָּנִ֔ים מֵרִפְי֖וֹן יָדָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: At the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his strong horses, at the rushing of his chariots, and at the rumbling of his wheels, the fathers shall not look back to their children for feebleness of hands;

verses.latin_vulgate: pro adventu diei in quo vastabuntur omnes Philisthiim,  et dissipabitur Tyrus et Sidon  cum omnibus reliquis auxiliis suis :  depopulatus est enim Dominus Palæstinos,  reliquias insulæ Cappadociæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַל־הַיּ֗וֹם הַבָּא֙ לִשְׁד֣וֹד אֶת־כׇּל־פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים לְהַכְרִ֤ית לְצֹר֙ וּלְצִיד֔וֹן כֹּ֖ל שָׂרִ֣יד עֹזֵ֑ר כִּֽי־שֹׁדֵ֤ד יְהֹוָה֙ אֶת־פְּלִשְׁתִּ֔ים שְׁאֵרִ֖ית אִ֥י כַפְתּֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: Because of the day that cometh to spoil all the Philistines, and to cut off from Tyrus and Zidon every helper that remaineth: for the Lord will spoil the Philistines, the remnant of the country of Caphtor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit calvitium super Gazam ;  conticuit Ascalon, et reliquiæ vallis earum :  usquequo concideris ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּ֤אָה קׇרְחָה֙ אֶל־עַזָּ֔ה נִדְמְתָ֥ה אַשְׁקְל֖וֹן שְׁאֵרִ֣ית עִמְקָ֑ם עַד־מָתַ֖י תִּתְגּוֹדָֽדִי׃
verses.king_james: Baldness is come upon Gaza; Ashkelon is cut off with the remnant of their valley: how long wilt thou cut thyself?

verses.latin_vulgate: O mucro Domini, usquequo non quiesces ?  ingredere in vaginam tuam,  refrigerare, et sile.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֗וֹי חֶ֚רֶב לַיהֹוָ֔ה עַד־אָ֖נָה לֹ֣א תִשְׁקֹ֑טִי הֵאָֽסְפִי֙ אֶל־תַּעְרֵ֔ךְ הֵרָגְעִ֖י וָדֹֽמִּי׃
verses.king_james: O thou sword of the Lord, how long will it be ere thou be quiet? put up thyself into thy scabbard, rest, and be still.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo quiescet,  cum Dominus præceperit ei adversus Ascalonem,  et adversus maritimas ejus regiones,  ibique condixerit illi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֣יךְ תִּשְׁקֹ֔טִי וַיהֹוָ֖ה צִוָּה־לָ֑הּ אֶֽל־אַשְׁקְל֛וֹן וְאֶל־ח֥וֹף הַיָּ֖ם שָׁ֥ם יְעָדָֽהּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: How can it be quiet, seeing the Lord hath given it a charge against Ashkelon, and against the sea shore? there hath he appointed it.
